title: agrostis hyemalis var. geminata
intro: Agrostis hyemalis var. geminata, also known as winter bentgrass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Agrostis hyemalis var. geminata include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
